model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: This is essentially a coin-flip between the top two teams in the country, but Duke gets the slight nod because the overall efficiency profile is still No. 1 on both KenPom (AdjEM 39.17) and Torvik, pairing a top-5 offense with the nation’s best defense. Michigan is right there (KenPom/Torvik No. 2) with a similarly elite defense, so the underdog path is very credible if Michigan wins the shot-variance battle and keeps Duke out of transition. With Duke’s only clearly confirmed absence remaining Caleb Foster (foot fracture) and no new, matchup-flipping availability news found for Michigan, I’ll lean to Duke’s marginally higher two-way baseline in a tight title game.

reasoningStep.title: National championship pick: Duke over Michigan
reasoningStep.summary: Pick Duke in a near toss-up based on a razor-thin but consistent edge in overall efficiency and defensive ceiling, with no new injury news that materially shifts either team’s baseline.
evidence: KenPom: Duke #1 (AdjEM 39.17; AdjO 127.9 #4; AdjD 88.7) vs Michigan #2 (AdjEM 38.38; AdjO 126.7 #8; AdjD 88.4).
Torvik: Duke #1 (AdjO rank 4; AdjD rank 1) vs Michigan #2 (AdjO rank 7; AdjD rank 2).
Availability: Duke guard Caleb Foster reported out indefinitely with a foot fracture (ESPN/AP); no newer report found indicating a return for the championship.
Underdog path: Michigan’s elite defense can win if it controls tempo, limits Duke’s transition/paint scoring, and wins the turnover + 3-point variance battle.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
